_comment: Plan 2 T15 — Veteran archetype dialog. Three archetypes: war_weary, proud, philosophical. Loaded by CompanionDialogueCatalog. Veteran is per_lord (released on discharge), so dialog frames service around the current commander.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Another fresh {PLAYER_RANK} in the line. I've buried more of those than I care to count. Try not to be the next, {PLAYER_NAME}.

options.text: I'll try not to give you reason to bury me, old friend.
I'll do the burying, not the dying.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: So you've come to learn from the best, {PLAYER_NAME}. Stand straight — a {PLAYER_RANK} under my eye carries himself with iron in his spine.

options.text: An honor to serve alongside you.
I'll prove myself in the line, not in the talking.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Fresh blood for {LORD_NAME}'s banner. Tell me, {PLAYER_NAME} — do you know yet why you carry a sword, or are you still finding out?

options.text: I'm still finding out. I think we all are.
I carry it because the lord pays me to.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Aye. We'll see how the seasons treat you, {PLAYER_NAME}. They're not gentle.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Good answer, {PLAYER_RANK}. Watch me in the line and you'll learn what a soldier is supposed to look like.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: An honest answer is rarer than a good sword, {PLAYER_NAME}. We'll talk again, when there's time.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Got time for an old soldier, {PLAYER_NAME}?

options.text: Tell me what you remember most.
What would you tell a young soldier?
What did you make of the last fight?
What do you make of the lord we serve?

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: I remember the faces, {PLAYER_NAME}. Not the victories — the faces. Boys I sat watch with, who never made it home. The fields blur. The faces don't.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: The wall at Praven. We took it before the sun was up — banner planted on the parapet while their lord was still in his nightshirt. That's the day they'll put on my stone, {PLAYER_RANK}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Most battles I've fought I cannot tell you why, {PLAYER_NAME}. Two lords disagreed and ten thousand men went to settle it. We march, we kill, we mourn — and the reason is always somewhere else, in a hall we'll never see.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Find one man in the line you'd die for, {PLAYER_NAME}, and stand next to him. The rest is noise. And keep a letter to your kin in your pack — write it before each battle, not after.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Train your arms when the others are sleeping. Polish your kit when the others are drinking. Be the {PLAYER_RANK} they call for when the line breaks. The rest takes care of itself.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Decide early what you will not do, {PLAYER_NAME}. The killing is easy — every soldier learns it. The line you will not cross is what makes you a man instead of a sword. Find it before someone else does it for you.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: We won. Three of theirs went down close enough I saw their eyes. Younger than you, {PLAYER_NAME}. Their mothers will hear about it next month, if at all.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Their line broke on the second push. I told you it would. A clean charge, a clean win — that's how a {PLAYER_RANK} earns his bread under {LORD_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Another field, another set of widows on each side. I cannot tell you whose cause was the just one, {PLAYER_NAME}. Perhaps neither. Perhaps both. The dead don't argue.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: {LORD_NAME}'s better than some I've served, worse than a few. Sends men forward when he should hold, holds when he should send. That's most of them, {PLAYER_NAME}. We'll bury what we must and march on.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: {LORD_NAME} has steel in him — I've watched him in the line. A real commander, not a hall-warmer. I'd ride with him through worse than this, and I have. You're a {PLAYER_RANK} I'd stand beside under that banner.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: {LORD_NAME} fights for what he was born to fight for. That's no small thing — most men do less. Whether his cause is justice or merely habit, only the years will tell. I serve him while the contract holds, {PLAYER_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Walk easy, {PLAYER_NAME}. The road's long.
